key_insight: Ultrasound- and microwave-assisted casein/gum arabic complexes combined with niosome encapsulation enabled an electrosprayed multilayer coating that significantly improved Bacillus coagulans viability.
engineering_relevance: The study shows a scalable ambient-temperature microencapsulation route for protecting sensitive probiotic payloads, useful for food and pharmaceutical processing workflows.
method: Ultrasonically pre-treated casein/gum arabic complexes followed by microwave treatment at 150, 350, and 550 W.
Prepared probiotic nanoniosomes using Span 20 and Tween 80, then loaded them into casein/gum arabic electrospraying solutions.
Applied electrospraying to form coated probiotic nanoparticles and verified structure and interactions with FTIR, rheology, and TEM.
evidence_signal: The 350 W microwave condition produced the strongest glycation/thermal stability, and 15% casein/gum arabic with Span 20 nanoniosomes achieved the highest post-electrospraying viability (97.85 ± 1.64%).
watch_item: Microwave power, formulation concentration, and surfactant choice appear highly sensitive, so scale-up will require careful process control to preserve viability and coating quality.
paper_title: Layer-by-layer coating of electrosprayed Bacillus coagulans using niosomes and casein/gum arabic complexes induced by ultrasound and microwave-assisted interactions
paper_category: AI materials, welding, and machine design
